experiment: what-happened-when readout through a frozen LLM (content-binding language capstone)
finding: Both fields are individually readable from the bound cortex code through the frozen LLM, but a JOINT single answer ('<...> <...>') has a CAPACITY TRADEOFF: the LLM reads whichever field training emphasizes, and what/when trade off.
separate_query: PENDING (notebooks/m4b_separate_readout_kaggle.py): ask WHAT or WHEN as separate queries so they never compete in one answer; expect BOTH ON>>OFF simultaneously, isolating the bottleneck to the joint-answer format, not the binding.
interpretation: The cortex BINDS both (CPU decode: what 100%, when 1.31 steps). Through the frozen-LLM fusion the categorical (what) and scalar (when) fields are each individually readable (WHAT sig event-first; WHEN sig time-first+upweighted) but trade off in a single joint answer -- a readout capacity bottleneck, distinct from the binding itself.
